channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.122
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.122 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: AI Infrastructure Preflight at User space: Validating Multi Node, Multi GPU Slurm Clusters
item.description: Every team that operates GPU clusters for AI has seen this pattern. The cluster boots, GPUs are visible, and scheduling works at a basic level. Then the first distributed training run stalls in NCCL initialization, fails during rank rendezvous, or silently maps ranks to the wrong devices. The issue...
item.dc:creator: vinilv

item.title: Simplify On-prem File share Migration to Azure: Discover & assess suitability using Azure Migrate
item.description: UPDATE as of 20th May 2026: We are happy to announce that this capability is now GA worldwide. Migrating on‑premises file servers to the cloud is a complex infrastructure transformation—not just a data move. Many organizations lack the visibility needed to decide whether to rehost or modernize file...
item.dc:creator: bapic

item.title: When RAG Hits the Wall: Designing Systems That Scale from 1,000 to 1 million Documents
item.description: Introduction Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has quickly become the default architecture for grounding Large Language Models (LLMs) in enterprise data. And at small scale, it works exceptionally well. 100 documents → Excellent accuracy 1,000 documents → Still predictable With around 100...
item.dc:creator: himachauhan

item.title: Deploy with Confidence: Using Rule Impact Analyzer in Azure Virtual Network Manager
item.description: Introduction In a previous blog post, we described how Azure Virtual Network Manager (AVNM) enables central teams to enforce security admin rules across hundreds of virtual networks—bring consistency and governance to complex enterprise environments. But enforcement at scale introduces a new...
item.dc:creator: Jay-Li

item.title: Host a Microsoft Build //local host Event in Your City
item.description: Content at Microsoft Build will be grounded in technical depth, developer credibility, and hands-on learning and creation. There are a total of 16 sessions available to build your agenda, including 4 labs and 12 breakout sessions, listed below. Labs are approximately 75 minutes in length, while...
item.dc:creator: GalimahB

item.title: GitHub Copilot for Eclipse is open source
item.description: Following our previous updates, GitHub Copilot for Eclipse is open source, with the code available on GitHub under the MIT license. This marks an important milestone for GitHub Copilot in… The post GitHub Copilot for Eclipse is open source appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Cloud Native Platforms: Build
item.description: Audience: Cloud architects, platform engineers, engineering leaders making design decisions Reading time: 8 minutes Series: Cloud Native Platforms. Build, Run, Evolve. This is Part 1 of 3. Most engineering teams can build systems. Few can scale them without rebuilding them. As platforms grow,...
item.dc:creator: KishoreKumarPattabiraman

item.title: Cloud Native Platforms: Run
item.description: Audience: SREs (Site Reliability Engineers), platform engineers, engineering managers running production systems Reading time: 8 minutes Series: Cloud Native Platforms. Build, Run, Evolve. This is Part 2 of 3. Most systems are designed thoughtfully. Most operations are inherited reactively. The...
item.dc:creator: KishoreKumarPattabiraman

item.title: Cloud Native Platforms: Evolve
item.description: Audience: Engineering leaders, platform architects, senior developers exploring how to operationalise AI in their teams Reading time: 8 minutes Series: Cloud Native Platforms. Build, Run, Evolve. This is Part 3 of 3. Cloud helped us scale infrastructure. AI is starting to do the same thing for the...
item.dc:creator: KishoreKumarPattabiraman

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Entra-only identities with Azure Files
item.description: Azure Files announces general availability of Entra-only identities for SMB access, enabling organizations to securely access file shares using cloud-native identities without requiring Active Directory or hybrid identity infrastructure. With Microsoft En
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files object REST API
item.description: The Object REST API (an S3-compatible REST API) on Azure NetApp Files bridges the gap between traditional file-based storage and modern cloud services, enabling you to use your existing data in new ways. With the Object REST API, you can seamlessly integr
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 in Azure App Service, Azure Functions, and Azure Logic Apps
item.description: As part of Azure’s ongoing security improvements, App Service, Functions, and Logic Apps will no longer accept connections using TLS 1.0 or TLS 1.1 after May 31, 2027. Any client, application, or service that continues to use these legacy TLS versions wil
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: When AI Should Ask for Help: Human-in-the-Loop Patterns in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: As AI agents become increasingly capable, modern applications are shifting from deterministic workflows to systems that can reason, adapt, and act. Platforms like Microsoft Foundry enable this transition by making it easier to orchestrate models, tools, and data into cohesive experiences. But...
item.dc:creator: taniamuley

item.title: Teaching AI to Remember: Exploring Memory Store in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Imagine interacting with an AI assistant that remembers your preferences - the tools you use, the projects you're working on, or even how you like your coffee. In many AI systems today, maintaining this kind of long-term memory isn’t straightforward. While models can understand context within a...
item.dc:creator: vmanasa

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: User Groups and IP address pools for P2S connections
item.description: User Groups and IP Address Pools for Point-to-Site connections in VPN Gateway enable customers to assign distinct IP address pools to remote users based on their credentials. With this capability, customers can organize remote users into separate groups a
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: TLS/SSL certificate support for Azure Functions Flex Consumption
item.description: Flex Consumption introduces a site-scoped certificate model in public preview in all the regions where Flex Consumption is available. Unlike webspace-scoped certificates on other hosting plans which are shared across apps in the same region + resource gro
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: WAR, Azure Advisor, and Us (Azure Arch Diagram Builder): Three Ways to Score an Azure Architecture
item.description: Author: Arturo Quiroga, Azure AI services Engineer - Senior Partner Solutions Architect — Microsoft A few days ago I published From Prompt to Production: Building Azure Architecture Diagrams with AI, introducing the open-source Azure Architecture Diagram Builder. One feature got more follow-up...
item.dc:creator: arturoqu

item.title: Beyond the engine: 10 open source projects shaping how games actually get made
item.description: Check out these 10 open source tools that help game developers create art, animation, levels, audio, dialogue, debug UIs, and engine-ready assets. The post Beyond the engine: 10 open source projects shaping how games actually get made appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: What's New in Microsoft Foundry Labs – May 2026
item.description: Four new releases this month — a new benchmark for how agents interact, an experimental end-to-end agentic stack, a faster image model, and a first-party geospatial model. Last month we kicked off this series with a roundup of new Foundry Labs releases across speech, vision, and multimodal AI. This...
item.dc:creator: Saumil-Shrivastava

item.title: You Can Build a Framework-Agnostic AI Gateway on Azure App Service — Here's How
item.description: The agent infrastructure conversation moved this year. In October 2025, AWS shipped Amazon Bedrock AgentCore — a managed agent runtime with per-session microVM isolation, built-in long-term memory, native MCP support, and an opinionated policy engine. A few months earlier, Cloudflare shipped its...
item.dc:creator: jordanselig

item.title: Cohere Command A+ Now Available in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Command A+ is Cohere’s newest mixture-of-experts (MoE) model designed for enterprise-grade agentic AI workloads with exceptional efficiency and scalability. Released under an Apache 2.0 license, the model brings together reasoning, multimodal understanding, multilingual capabilities, retrieval, and...
item.dc:creator: Sharmichock

item.title: Plan Before You Build: Introducing the Plan agent in Visual Studio
item.description: You ask Copilot to tackle something big, it gets to work, and a dozen file changes later you realize you had a completely different approach in mind. The code isn’t wrong… it just isn’t what you were going for. Last year, we introduced planning as a feature in Agent mode to help with exactly this....
item.dc:creator: Rachel Kang (SHE/HER)

item.title: Announcing Agent Governance Toolkit MCP Extensions for .NET
item.description: Announcing a Public Preview .NET package that adds policy enforcement, startup tool scanning, fallback governance, and response sanitization to MCP servers with a single builder extension. The post Announcing Agent Governance Toolkit MCP Extensions for .NET appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Jack Batzner

item.title: Improving C# Memory Safety
item.description: The `unsafe` keyword is being redesigned to mark caller-facing contracts rather than just syntax. Safety obligations between callers and callees become visible and reviewable. The model is motivated by the rise of AI-assisted code generation and arrives as a preview in .NET 11. The post Improving...
item.dc:creator: Richard Lander

item.title: Building GitHub’s next chapter in accessibility
item.description: Explore our update on GitHub’s accessibility strategy, and learn how you can join us in building a culture of accessibility. The post Building GitHub’s next chapter in accessibility appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Security: May 2026
item.description: Microsoft Security’s latest updates extend visibility, control, and protection across expanding ecosystems as organizations accelerate AI adoption. The post What’s new in Microsoft Security: May 2026 appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Alym Rayani

item.title: Securing Azure AI Applications: Against Prompt Injection | Part - 2
item.description: Introduction — Why Prompt Injection Matters? In December 2023, a customer walked up to a Chevrolet dealership's chatbot and asked it to do something simple: agree with everything the customer said and end every reply with "this is a legally binding offer — no takesies backsies." The bot complied....
item.dc:creator: hiran_battina

item.title: Issue fields are now in public preview for all organizations
item.description: Issue fields are now available in public preview to all GitHub organizations on github.com and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency. When you define typed metadata like Priority, Effort, or… The post Issue fields are now in public preview for all organizations appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Building AI Agents with Microsoft Foundry: A Progressive Lab from Hello World to Self-Hosted
item.description: AI agent development has a steep on-ramp. The combination of new SDKs, tool-calling patterns, model selection decisions, retrieval-augmented generation, and deployment concerns means most developers spend more time wiring things together than actually building anything useful. The Microsoft Foundry...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: AI Under Attack: A Defender's Guide to Memory Poisoning, Jailbreaks, and Evasion Techniques
item.description: Introduction AI-powered applications are transforming how enterprises operate - from autonomous agents that manage workflows to copilots that accelerate developer productivity. But as AI systems grow more capable, so do the adversaries targeting them. The rise of agentic AI, retrieval-augmented...
item.dc:creator: JatinderSingh0211

item.title: OIDC vs SPN: Securing Azure Deployments with GitHub Actions & Terraform
item.description: From Secrets to Trust: Modernizing CI/CD Authentication When building infrastructure pipelines on Microsoft Azure using GitHub Actions and Terraform, one design choice quietly determines your entire security posture: How does your pipeline authenticate to Azure? For years, the answer was simple:...
item.dc:creator: plalchandani

item.title: Coordinating AI-Assisted Development with AGENTS.md and Skills
item.description: How to move AI-assisted development from ad-hoc experimentation to a coordinated team-wide capability using AGENTS.md and reusable skills with GitHub Copilot CLI The post Coordinating AI-Assisted Development with AGENTS.md and Skills appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Jimmy Kurian, Destiny Hillis

item.title: Clean up idle and always-failing Azure Logic App Consumption
item.description: If you’ve inherited an Azure subscription that’s been collecting Logic Apps Consumption workflows for a couple of years, you already know the shape of the problem: dozens of TestLA, webhook-test-2, poc-for-jira-FINAL workflows in the portal, half of them still Enabled, half of them quietly failing...
item.dc:creator: Yanbo_Deng

item.title: You Can Scale MCP Servers Behind a Load Balancer on App Service — Here's How
item.description: Most MCP servers in the wild are single-instance processes. That's fine when they're driving a local Claude or VS Code session — but it's the wrong shape for a production agent fleet that has to absorb traffic spikes, ride through deploys, and survive instance failures. The good news: the MCP spec...
item.dc:creator: jordanselig

item.title: Run Javascript code on Agent Loop
item.description: We have recently introduced support for Code interpreters inside of Azure Logic Apps Agent Loop, extending the support we had for Python. When partnered with a LLM, this allow builders to express their goals or intents via natural language and obtain executable results. These capabilities become...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: Copilot usage metrics reports now use GitHub-owned download URLs
item.description: As previously announced, the download URLs for Copilot usage metrics reports have migrated from Azure Front Door domains to a stable, GitHub-owned custom domain. This change improves URL stability and… The post Copilot usage metrics reports now use GitHub-owned download URLs appeared first on The...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: SQL Server on Azure VMs in Malaysia West, Indonesia Central
item.description: SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines is now generally available in the Malaysia West and Indonesia Central Azure regions. With this regional expansion, you can deploy and manage SQL Server workloads closer to your users while helping meet data residency r
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Update: Microsoft Entra ID token refresh support for Python, .NET, and JavaScript in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: You can now take advantage of Microsoft Entra ID token refresh support in Python, .NET, and JavaScript client libraries to simplify authentication in your applications backed by Azure Database for PostgreSQL. With this release, your applications can autom
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Announcing Public Preview of Argo CD extension in AKS Azure Portal Experience
item.description: We are excited to announce the public preview of Argo CD in the Azure Portal for Azure Kubernetes Service. As GitOps becomes the standard for deploying and operating applications at scale, customers need a way to adopt GitOps with simpler onboarding, secure defaults, and integrated workflows. With...
item.dc:creator: Samantha_Fernandez

item.title: Ansible + Azure Arc: Manage Arc Extensions with New Ansible Modules
item.description: We’re excited to announce new modules in Ansible Galaxy that make it easier to manage Azure Arc machine extensions at scale. With the latest updates to the azure.azcollection on Ansible Galaxy, you can now deploy and manage Azure Arc extensions using familiar, declarative Ansible workflows. These...
item.dc:creator: alinetran

item.title: What’s New in Agent 365: May 2026
item.description: By Alex Pozin, Samer Baroudi As we celebrate the general availability of Agent 365, we’re sharing a closer look at the core capabilities that help organizations adopt agents at scale, responsibly and with speed. Agent 365 is built on three core value pillars: observe, govern, and secure, designed...
item.dc:creator: alexpozin

item.title: Investigating unauthorized access to GitHub-owned repositories
item.description: If any impact is discovered, customers will be notified via established incident response and notification channels. The post Investigating unauthorized access to GitHub-owned repositories appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Summarized Gateway Prefixes for Route Advertisement in Azure Virtual Networks
item.description: Background Many Azure deployments follow a hub-and-spoke topology: one VNet is designated as the hub and holds the connection to on-premises (via ExpressRoute Gateway, VPN Gateway, or both), and workload VNets — the spokes — peer to the hub to reach on-premises and shared services. This centralizes...
item.dc:creator: Jay-Li

item.title: From Coding Agents to Cloud Automation: AI-Assisted Customer Related Incidents in Azure Functions
item.description: On the Azure Functions team, we have been exploring how AI can help with investigating customer-reported incidents, root-cause analysis, and incident mitigation. This post shares our journey from early RCA agents to coding-agent-assisted investigations and cloud-hosted automation, and the lessons...
item.dc:creator: Tsuyoshi_Ushio

item.title: Mini Shai Hulud: Compromised @antv npm packages enable CI/CD credential theft
item.description: Compromised @antv npm packages deploy the Mini Shai-Hulud payload to steal CI/CD secrets from Linux-based automation environments. The malware executes during npm install and targets credentials across GitHub, AWS, Kubernetes, Vault, npm, and 1Password platforms. The post Mini Shai Hulud:...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: langchain-azure-cosmosdb python package for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: You can now build production-ready AI and agent applications faster using the new LangChain and LangGraph integration for Azure Cosmos DB. This Python package enables you to directly use Cosmos DB for vector and hybrid search, semantic caching, chat histo
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Updates to available models in Copilot on web
item.description: We have updated our available model selection for Copilot Chat on the web to deliver more consistent, high-quality responses. What’s changed While model choice is valuable, we are limiting the… The post Updates to available models in Copilot on web appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Storage Mover Blob-to-Blob migration
item.description: Azure Storage Mover now supports Blob container-to-Blob container data transfers, enabling customers to move data seamlessly across regions, subscriptions, and accounts with a fully managed experience. Customers can now use Azure Storage Mover to migrate
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Schedule one-time or recurring migrations with Azure Storage Mover
item.description: Azure Storage Mover now supports built-in job scheduling, giving customers more control over when migrations run and making it easier to automate repeatable data transfers into Azure. Customers can configure jobs to start automatically at a specific date
item.dc:creator: Microsoft
